payload.source_text:
QUALITY POLICY STATEMENT
Novus Mechanical Services Limited has established a Quality Policy to be consistent with the purpose and context of our organisation.

It provides a framework for the setting and review of objectives in addition to our commitment to satisfy applicable customers’, regulatory and legislative requirements as well as our commitment to continually improve our management system.
Customer focus: As an organisation we have made a commitment to understand our current and future customers’ needs; meet their requirements and strive to exceed their expectations.
Leadership: Our Top Management have committed to creating and maintaining a working environment in which people become fully involved in achieving our objectives.
Engagement of people: As an organisation we recognise that people are the essence of any good business and that their full involvement enables their abilities to be used for our benefit.

Process approach: As an organisation we understand that a desired result is achieved more efficiently when activities and related resources are managed as a process or series of interconnected processes.
Improvement: We have committed to achieving continual improvement across all aspects of our quality management system; it is one of our main annual objectives.
Evidence-based decision making: As an organisation we have committed to only make decisions relating to our QMS following an analysis of relevant data and information.

Relationship management: We recognises that an organisation and the relationship it has with its external providers are interdependent and a mutually beneficial relationship enhances the ability of both to create value.
Our policy is also to meet the requirements of our social, environmental, charitable and legislative responsibilities.

Signed : 							Date: 22/4/2025
           	
Damian Shacklock
Director
NOVUS MECHANICAL SERVICES LTD.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Source elements present in the ledger.

payload.evaluated_scope: All input material in this project

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Establishment of quality objectives aligned with policy framework
payload.justification: Objective-setting represents a strategic function that operationalizes the quality policy across the enterprise.

payload.description: Quality management system execution using process approach methodology
payload.justification: Process approach represents the strategic methodology for achieving organizational quality results.

payload.description: Continual improvement program across quality management system
payload.justification: Continual improvement represents an ongoing strategic process that spans the quality management system annually.

payload.description: Mandatory compliance with applicable regulatory requirements
payload.justification: Regulatory compliance represents a strategic rule that constrains and governs organizational behavior.

payload.description: Quality management system operation encompassing policy establishment, objective-setting, and compliance activities
payload.justification: Quality management represents the enterprise-level process for governing quality across the organization

payload.description: Continuous improvement capability applied systematically across quality management domains
payload.justification: Continual improvement is a core organizational function performed repeatedly as a capability

payload.description: Evidence-based decision making capability for quality governance
payload.justification: Decision making based on evidence represents a repeatable organizational function

payload.description: Process approach methodology mandated as organizational standard for achieving operational results
payload.justification: Process approach represents a governing rule that constrains how work must be performed

payload.description: Mandatory compliance with regulatory and legislative requirements within quality operations
payload.justification: Regulatory compliance represents a binding rule that governs organizational processes

payload.description: Quality policy framework serving as foundational governance structure for organizational quality management
payload.justification: Quality policy represents a strategic entity that governs organizational quality approach. Signals describe it as foundational governance framework with defined purpose.

payload.description: Customer expectations and requirements as strategic information assets
payload.justification: Customer needs and expectations represent strategic data entities that drive organizational objectives and performance.

payload.description: External provider relationships as strategic partnership assets
payload.justification: Provider relationships are strategic entities that create value and represent important organizational assets at the planner level.

payload.description: Alignment between organizational quality policy and business context
payload.justification: The signal explicitly describes alignment relationship between quality policy and business purpose/context as strategic connection.

payload.description: Quality policy framework serving as the foundational governance instrument for organizational quality management
payload.justification: Quality policy is a strategic artifact (thing) that embodies governance structure and framework for the enterprise

payload.description: Customer needs and expectations as strategic information assets requiring organizational understanding
payload.justification: Customer needs represent strategic data entities that the organization must capture and manage

payload.description: Work environment as organizational infrastructure enabling objective achievement
payload.justification: Work environment represents a tangible organizational asset at the strategic level

payload.description: External provider relationships as strategic partnership assets
payload.justification: Provider relationships are strategic entities representing organizational connections for value creation

payload.description: People engagement level as measurable organizational capability indicator
payload.justification: Engagement represents a quality characteristic of the organizational workforce entity

payload.description: Annual quality management improvement objectives cycle
payload.justification: Annual cycle represents the temporal rhythm for continual improvement objectives at strategic level.

payload.description: Annual quality management review and objective-setting cycle
payload.justification: Annual objectives indicate a recurring strategic planning cycle

payload.description: Policy authorization milestone establishing governance authority
payload.justification: Policy authorization represents a significant governance event with temporal importance

payload.description: Organizational leadership accountable for enabling work environment and quality objectives
payload.justification: Leadership role is identified as strategically responsible for creating conditions for organizational success.

payload.description: Organizational director with formal authority over quality policy governance
payload.justification: Director role represents the governance authority with accountability for policy authorization at strategic level.

payload.description: Engaged workforce contributing to organizational objectives achievement
payload.justification: People/workforce represent strategic actors whose engagement enables business benefit realization.

payload.description: Customers whose needs and expectations drive organizational strategy
payload.justification: Customers are external strategic actors whose requirements influence organizational direction and performance.

payload.description: External providers engaged in mutually beneficial strategic partnerships
payload.justification: External providers represent organizations in strategic relationship with the enterprise for value creation.

payload.description: Organizational leadership responsible for creating enabling conditions and demonstrating commitment
payload.justification: Leadership represents a strategic role with accountability for organizational environment

payload.description: Director authority with formal governance responsibility for policy authorization
payload.justification: Director represents the executive role with formal authority and accountability

payload.description: Customers as external stakeholders whose needs and expectations drive organizational strategy
payload.justification: Customers are strategic actors who interact with and influence the enterprise

payload.description: Engaged workforce as internal stakeholders enabling organizational benefit realization
payload.justification: People represent the actor group whose engagement drives strategic outcomes

payload.description: External providers as strategic partner organizations in value creation ecosystem
payload.justification: External providers are organizational entities with whom strategic relationships are maintained

payload.description: Achievement of organizational quality and operational objectives
payload.justification: Organizational objectives represent strategic goals that provide direction and purpose for enterprise activities.

payload.description: Understanding and exceeding customer expectations to drive satisfaction
payload.justification: Customer satisfaction and expectation fulfillment represent strategic goals that drive organizational commitment.

payload.description: Creation of mutual value through strategic provider relationships
payload.justification: Value creation represents the strategic goal underlying provider relationship management.

payload.description: Continuous improvement as foundational quality management philosophy
payload.justification: Continual improvement represents a strategic principle that guides organizational approach to quality management.

payload.description: Process approach as fundamental methodology for organizational efficiency
payload.justification: Process approach represents a strategic principle that governs how the organization structures work for efficiency.

payload.description: Evidence-based decision making as governance standard for quality management
payload.justification: Evidence-based decision making represents a strategic principle that guides organizational governance approach.

payload.description: People engagement as strategic enabler for organizational benefit
payload.justification: People engagement represents a strategic principle recognizing human capital as essential organizational value.

payload.description: Mandatory fulfillment of social, environmental, charitable, and legislative responsibilities
payload.justification: These responsibilities represent strategic constraints that limit organizational freedom and mandate specific behaviors.

payload.description: Regulatory compliance requirements governing quality management operations
payload.justification: Regulatory requirements represent external constraints that must be satisfied and limit strategic options.

payload.description: Achievement of customer satisfaction through understanding and exceeding expectations
payload.justification: Customer satisfaction represents a strategic end-state the organization aims to achieve

payload.description: Realization of organizational objectives through enabling work environment and people engagement
payload.justification: Achievement of objectives is the stated purpose driving environment and engagement initiatives

payload.description: Value creation through strategic external partnerships
payload.justification: Value creation represents the desired outcome motivating provider relationship strategy

payload.description: Enhancement of organizational efficiency and result achievement through systematic process management
payload.justification: Efficiency and results represent the strategic motivations for adopting process approach

payload.description: Quality policy alignment with business purpose and organizational context as governance foundation
payload.justification: Alignment principle establishes how quality policy must relate to business purpose

payload.description: Continuous improvement as enduring organizational philosophy across all quality domains
payload.justification: Continual improvement represents a fundamental belief system guiding organizational behavior

payload.description: Evidence-based decision making as foundational governance philosophy
payload.justification: Evidence-based approach represents a core belief about how decisions should be made

payload.description: Process-oriented methodology as fundamental efficiency doctrine
payload.justification: Process approach as principle represents the underlying belief driving methodological choices

payload.description: People engagement as core organizational value and enabler philosophy
payload.justification: Recognition of people as essential value represents a guiding principle for organizational design

payload.description: Social, environmental, charitable, and legislative obligations limiting organizational discretion
payload.justification: These responsibilities represent external constraints that bound organizational action

payload.description: Regulatory compliance requirements establishing mandatory operational boundaries
payload.justification: Regulatory requirements constrain organizational freedom and mandate specific actions

payload.description: Customer Focus - understanding customer needs, fulfilling requirements, and surpassing expectations
payload.justification: Customer focus is described as a functional business capability involving specific activities of understanding, meeting, and exceeding.

payload.description: People Engagement - activating and leveraging human capabilities as organizational resources
payload.justification: People engagement represents a functional capability to recognize and utilize human assets within the organization.

payload.description: Process Approach - managing interconnected activities and resources as integrated process systems
payload.justification: Explicitly defined as a process model for managing activities and resources through systematic interconnection.

payload.description: Continual Improvement - ongoing enhancement of quality management system performance
payload.justification: Continual improvement is characterized as an ongoing process applied across system aspects rather than a one-time activity.

payload.description: Relationship Management - cultivating and maintaining interdependent partnerships with external providers
payload.justification: Described as an active management process for handling external relationships to create mutual value.

payload.description: Evidence-based decisions required - quality management system decisions must be grounded in analyzed data
payload.justification: Explicitly established as a business rule mandating data analysis as prerequisite to QMS decision-making.

payload.description: Managing activities and resources as interconnected processes to achieve efficiency
payload.justification: Signal establishes process approach as a conceptual model for managing workflows and resource transformation.

payload.description: Understanding customer needs and meeting their requirements while exceeding expectations
payload.justification: These are distinct business functions that define customer-focused operational activities.

payload.description: Creating organizational environments that enable people to achieve objectives
payload.justification: This represents a leadership function focused on environmental enablement at the business level.

payload.description: Quality management decisions must be preceded by data analysis and factual evidence
payload.justification: Signal explicitly establishes this as a business rule constraining decision-making process.

payload.description: Quality Policy - formal statement defining organizational quality commitments and framework
payload.justification: Quality policy represents a core business entity containing structured information about organizational quality intentions. SG447 and SG448 together establish it as a conceptual artifact with defined scope and components.

payload.description: Customer Requirements - documented needs and expectations from customers
payload.justification: Customer requirements are distinct business data objects referenced in policy framework and customer focus concepts, representing essential input information.

payload.description: Quality Management System - integrated framework of policies, processes, and practices
payload.justification: The QMS is referenced as a distinct business entity with aspects subject to improvement and decision-making processes.

payload.description: External Provider Relationships - formal business connections with suppliers and partners
payload.justification: External provider relationships are identified as distinct business entities that require management and create mutual value.

payload.description: Organizational Context - environmental and situational factors influencing quality policy
payload.justification: Context serves as a qualifying attribute that shapes quality policy formulation, representing environmental characteristics.

payload.description: Policy encompasses Objectives - quality policy contains and defines quality objectives
payload.justification: The policy framework explicitly encompasses objective-setting, establishing a containment relationship between these entities.

payload.description: Processes are interconnected - business processes link together to form systematic workflows
payload.justification: The process approach explicitly defines interconnection as a fundamental relationship between process entities.

payload.description: Quality Policy as a documented business entity defining organizational quality commitments and direction
payload.justification: Quality policy is a core business artifact that captures organizational quality commitments. Signals describe it as conceptual foundation and policy framework.

payload.description: Customer as a business entity with identifiable needs, requirements, and expectations
payload.justification: Customer represents a distinct business entity at the conceptual level with associated attributes (needs, requirements, expectations).

payload.description: External Provider as a business entity engaged in interdependent value-creating relationships
payload.justification: External providers are distinct business entities characterized by relationship-based value exchange.

payload.description: Interdependent value creation relationship between organization and external providers
payload.justification: Signal explicitly identifies this as a conceptual model of interdependent relationships that mutually create value.

payload.description: Customer needs, requirements, and expectations as defining attributes
payload.justification: These represent measurable attributes of the customer entity in the business context.

payload.description: Continual improvement as an ongoing cycle applied across all quality management aspects
payload.justification: Continual improvement represents a perpetual business cycle rather than a discrete event.

payload.description: Leadership - accountable party for establishing enabling environments and facilitating objective achievement
payload.justification: Leadership is defined with specific responsibilities and accountabilities, representing a distinct business role.

payload.description: People - human resources whose capabilities constitute essential organizational assets
payload.justification: People are identified as active participants and asset holders within the business context.

payload.description: Customers - parties with needs, requirements, and expectations to be satisfied
payload.justification: Customers are identified as external actors whose needs drive business activities.

payload.description: External Providers - independent parties in value-creating relationships with the organization
payload.justification: External providers are distinct actors participating in interdependent business relationships.

payload.description: Leadership role responsible for creating enabling environments and driving objective achievement
payload.justification: Signal defines leadership as conceptual responsibility, indicating a business role with specific accountabilities.

payload.description: People as organizational actors whose capabilities and engagement constitute essential assets
payload.justification: People are characterized as actors with capabilities who perform within the organizational context.

payload.description: Achieve quality objectives aligned with organizational purpose
payload.justification: Quality policy establishes objective-setting as a central goal, providing purpose and direction for the organization.

payload.description: Continuously improve quality management system effectiveness
payload.justification: Continual improvement is explicitly identified as a central business objective across all system aspects.

payload.description: Create mutual value through external provider partnerships
payload.justification: Value creation is established as the purpose behind relationship management with external providers.

payload.description: Customer focus drives business decisions and activities
payload.justification: Customer focus is established as a foundational business principle guiding organizational behavior.

payload.description: Leadership enables achievement through people involvement
payload.justification: The leadership concept articulates a fundamental principle about how organizational goals are achieved.

payload.description: Human capabilities are essential organizational assets requiring engagement
payload.justification: The recognition of people as essential assets represents a core business principle guiding resource management.

payload.description: Process interconnection enhances organizational efficiency
payload.justification: The process approach establishes efficiency through interconnection as a guiding principle for organizational design.

payload.description: Decisions must be evidence-based and data-driven
payload.justification: Evidence-based decision making represents a fundamental principle governing how organizational choices are made.

payload.description: Must comply with regulatory requirements
payload.justification: Regulatory compliance is identified as a mandatory element within the policy framework, representing an external constraint.

payload.description: Must address social, environmental, charitable, and legislative responsibilities
payload.justification: Extended policy scope explicitly defines mandatory responsibility areas that constrain organizational operations.

payload.description: Achieve quality objectives aligned with organizational purpose and operational context
payload.justification: Signals establish objective-setting and achievement as central goals of the policy framework.

payload.description: Meet customer requirements and exceed customer expectations
payload.justification: These represent explicit business goals within the customer focus concept.

payload.description: Achieve continual improvement across all quality management system dimensions
payload.justification: Signal explicitly defines continual improvement as a central business objective.

payload.description: Comply with applicable regulatory, legislative, social, environmental, and charitable requirements
payload.justification: These represent mandatory constraints limiting organizational discretion in policy scope and execution.

payload.description: Customer focus as foundational principle requiring needs understanding and expectation fulfillment
payload.justification: Signal articulates customer focus as a core business concept that guides organizational behavior.

payload.description: People engagement as foundational principle recognizing human capabilities as strategic organizational assets
payload.justification: Signal identifies this as a core business concept establishing philosophical approach to human resources.

payload.description: Evidence-based decision making as governing principle requiring factual foundation for choices
payload.justification: While also a rule, this represents a fundamental principle governing organizational decision philosophy.

payload.description: Process approach as fundamental principle treating activities and resources as interconnected systems
payload.justification: Signal establishes this as a conceptual model that guides how the organization views its operations.

payload.description: Management of interconnected process series to transform resources into outcomes.
payload.justification: SG463 implies a logical process concept where activities and resources are managed through process series. This represents the functional 'how' of process management at the Designer abstraction level.

payload.description: Analysis of data and information to support decision-making activities.
payload.justification: SG464 implies a functional requirement to analyze data and information as part of the decision-making process. This represents a logical function within the QMS, distinct from the principle it supports.

payload.description: Logical process construct representing interconnected series of activities consuming resources to achieve organizational results
payload.justification: SG463 describes a process approach principle that defines the logical structure of how activities and resources are organized into processes. This maps to the How column as it represents logical process architecture at the Designer level.

payload.description: Management function to coordinate activities and resources through process-based methodology
payload.justification: The management philosophy articulated in SG463 represents a logical function for organizing work, distinct from the process construct itself.

payload.description: Decision-making process requiring analysis of data and information as input to QMS decisions
payload.justification: SG464 establishes a logical decision-making process that transforms data and information into organizational decisions within the QMS context.

payload.description: Dependency relationship between activities, resources, and achieved results within process constructs
payload.justification: SG463 implies a logical relationship structure connecting activities and resources to results. This represents the data relationships supporting process architecture.

payload.description: Analytical dependency relationship between data, information, and decision outputs in QMS
payload.justification: SG464 establishes a logical relationship where decisions depend on analyzed data and information, representing a fundamental data flow relationship at the Designer level.

payload.description: Activities and resources shall be managed through interconnected processes to achieve effective results.
payload.justification: SG463 explicitly identifies a principle regarding process approach management philosophy. This maps to the Why column as it establishes a design principle for how the system should conceptually operate at the logical level.

payload.description: Decisions within the quality management system must be grounded in analysis of data and information.
payload.justification: SG464 describes an evidence-based decision making principle that establishes a logical requirement for the QMS. This is a design principle governing decision-making behavior, appropriately classified in the Why column.

payload.description: Process approach principle constraining system design to organize activities and resources as interconnected processes
payload.justification: SG463 explicitly identifies a design principle that constrains how the logical architecture must be structured. The principle-based nature fits the Why column at Designer level.

payload.description: Evidence-based decision making principle requiring analytical foundation for all QMS decisions
payload.justification: SG464 explicitly establishes a principle governing decision-making behavior. This design principle constrains the logical decision process and belongs in the Why column.

payload.description: Temporal validity period attribute marking effective date range for policy enforcement
payload.justification: The temporal validity referenced in the signal represents a data attribute that defines the time-bounded effectiveness of a policy document. At the Implementer level, this is a detailed data specification defining validity constraints. Classified as Attribute rather than Event/Cycle because it describes a property of a policy entity rather than a temporal occurrence.

payload.description: Director role with documented signature authority for policy authorization and approval
payload.justification: Signal identifies Director as a specific role with signature authority over policy documents. At the Implementer level, this represents a detailed role specification with formal approval responsibilities. Classification as Role rather than Actor because it describes a position with specific organizational authority rather than a system interface or user type.

payload.description: Authorization constraint requiring Director-level signature validation for policy document enforceability
payload.justification: The signature requirement represents a detailed validation rule that constrains policy document validity. At the Implementer level, this is a specific business rule governing document authorization. Classified as Constraint because it defines a mandatory condition that must be satisfied for policy enforcement.

payload.description: Establishes organizational quality policy aligned with business purpose and context as foundational governance framework.
Defines quality policy framework purpose for objective-setting and commitment to regulatory compliance and continuous improvement.
Articulates strategic commitment to understanding customer needs and exceeding expectations as organizational driver.
Expresses leadership commitment to creating enabling work environment for achieving organizational objectives.
Recognizes people engagement as essential organizational value and strategic enabler for business benefit.
Declares process approach as organizational efficiency principle for achieving desired results.
Establishes continual improvement across quality management as core annual organizational objective.
Commits organization to evidence-based decision making principle for quality management governance.
Recognizes mutually beneficial external provider relationships as strategic driver for value creation.
Declares policy commitment to social, environmental, charitable, and legislative responsibilities as organizational obligations.
Provides formal policy authorization by organizational director establishing governance authority and accountability.
Establishes the conceptual foundation for a quality policy aligned with organizational purpose and context.
Defines the policy framework as encompassing objective-setting, customer requirements, regulatory compliance, and continual improvement commitments.
Articulates customer focus as a business concept requiring understanding of needs, meeting requirements, and exceeding expectations.
Defines leadership as the conceptual responsibility for creating environments that enable objective achievement through people involvement.
Identifies people engagement as a core business concept recognizing human capabilities as essential organizational assets.
Establishes process approach as a conceptual model where activities and resources are managed as interconnected processes for efficiency.
Defines continual improvement as a central business objective across all quality management system aspects.
Establishes evidence-based decision making as a business rule requiring data analysis before quality management system decisions.
Defines relationship management as a conceptual model of interdependent external provider relationships that mutually create value.
Extends policy scope to encompass social, environmental, charitable, and legislative responsibility requirements.
Process approach principle articulating logical management philosophy that activities and resources achieve results through process or interconnected process series.
Evidence-based decision making principle establishing logical requirement that QMS decisions follow data and information analysis.
Director signature block identifying specific organizational authority and temporal validity of policy document.
